NKJV.1: When morning came, all the chief priests and elders of the people plotted against Jesus to put Him to death.
NKJV.2: And when they had bound Him, they led Him away and delivered Him to Pontius Pilate the governor.
NKJV.3: Then Judas, His betrayer, seeing that He had been condemned, was remorseful and brought back the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders,
NKJV.4: saying, “I have sinned by betraying innocent blood.” And they said, “What is that to us? You see to it!”
NKJV.5: Then he threw down the pieces of silver in the temple and departed, and went and hanged himself.
NKJV.6: But the chief priests took the silver pieces and said, “It is not lawful to put them into the treasury, because they are the price of blood.”
NKJV.7: And they consulted together and bought with them the potter’s field, to bury strangers in.
NKJV.8: Therefore that field has been called the Field of Blood to this day.
NKJV.9: Then was fulfilled what was spoken by Jeremiah the prophet, saying, “And they took the thirty pieces of silver, the value of Him who was priced, whom they of the children of Israel priced,
NKJV.10: and gave them for the potter’s field, as the LORD directed me.”
NKJV.11: Now Jesus stood before the governor. And the governor asked Him, saying, “Are You the King of the Jews?” Jesus said to him, “It is as you say.”
NKJV.12: And while He was being accused by the chief priests and elders, He answered nothing.
NKJV.13: Then Pilate said to Him, “Do You not hear how many things they testify against You?”
NKJV.14: But He answered him not one word, so that the governor marveled greatly.
NKJV.15: Now at the feast the governor was accustomed to releasing to the multitude one prisoner whom they wished.
NKJV.16: And at that time they had a notorious prisoner called Barabbas.
NKJV.17: Therefore, when they had gathered together, Pilate said to them, “Whom do you want me to release to you? Barabbas, or Jesus who is called Christ?”
NKJV.18: For he knew that they had handed Him over because of envy.
NKJV.19: While he was sitting on the judgment seat, his wife sent to him, saying, “Have nothing to do with that just Man, for I have suffered many things today in a dream because of Him.”
NKJV.20: But the chief priests and elders persuaded the multitudes that they should ask for Barabbas and destroy Jesus.
NKJV.21: The governor answered and said to them, “Which of the two do you want me to release to you?” They said, “Barabbas!”
NKJV.22: Pilate said to them, “What then shall I do with Jesus who is called Christ?” They all said to him, “Let Him be crucified!”
NKJV.23: Then the governor said, “Why, what evil has He done?” But they cried out all the more, saying, “Let Him be crucified!”
NKJV.24: When Pilate saw that he could not prevail at all, but rather that a tumult was rising, he took water and washed his hands before the multitude, saying, “I am innocent of the blood of this just Person. You see to it.”
NKJV.25: And all the people answered and said, “His blood be on us and on our children.”
NKJV.26: Then he released Barabbas to them; and when he had scourged Jesus, he delivered Him to be crucified.
NKJV.27: Then the soldiers of the governor took Jesus into the Praetorium and gathered the whole garrison around Him.
NKJV.28: And they stripped Him and put a scarlet robe on Him.
NKJV.29: When they had twisted a crown of thorns, they put it on His head, and a reed in His right hand. And they bowed the knee before Him and mocked Him, saying, “Hail, King of the Jews!”
NKJV.30: Then they spat on Him, and took the reed and struck Him on the head.
NKJV.31: And when they had mocked Him, they took the robe off Him, put His own clothes on Him, and led Him away to be crucified.
NKJV.32: Now as they came out, they found a man of Cyrene, Simon by name. Him they compelled to bear His cross.
NKJV.33: And when they had come to a place called Golgotha, that is to say, Place of a Skull,
NKJV.34: they gave Him sour wine mingled with gall to drink. But when He had tasted it, He would not drink.
NKJV.35: Then they crucified Him, and divided His garments, casting lots, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet:  “They divided My garments among them, And for My clothing they cast lots.”
NKJV.36: Sitting down, they kept watch over Him there.
NKJV.37: And they put up over His head the accusation written against Him: THIS IS JESUS THE KING OF THE JEWS.
NKJV.38: Then two robbers were crucified with Him, one on the right and another on the left.
NKJV.39: And those who passed by blasphemed Him, wagging their heads
NKJV.40: and saying, “You who destroy the temple and build it in three days, save Yourself! If You are the Son of God, come down from the cross.”
NKJV.41: Likewise the chief priests also, mocking with the scribes and elders, said,
NKJV.42: “He saved others; Himself He cannot save. If He is the King of Israel, let Him now come down from the cross, and we will believe Him.
NKJV.43: He trusted in God; let Him deliver Him now if He will have Him; for He said, ‘I am the Son of God.’ ”
NKJV.44: Even the robbers who were crucified with Him reviled Him with the same thing.
NKJV.45: Now from the sixth hour until the ninth hour there was darkness over all the land.
NKJV.46: And about the ninth hour Jesus cried out with a loud voice, saying, “Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani?” that is,  “My God, My God, why have You forsaken Me?”
NKJV.47: Some of those who stood there, when they heard that, said, “This Man is calling for Elijah!”
NKJV.48: Immediately one of them ran and took a sponge, filled it with sour wine and put it on a reed, and offered it to Him to drink.
NKJV.49: The rest said, “Let Him alone; let us see if Elijah will come to save Him.”
NKJV.50: And Jesus cried out again with a loud voice, and yielded up His spirit.
NKJV.51: Then, behold, the veil of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom; and the earth quaked, and the rocks were split,
NKJV.52: and the graves were opened; and many bodies of the saints who had fallen asleep were raised;
NKJV.53: and coming out of the graves after His resurrection, they went into the holy city and appeared to many.
NKJV.54: So when the centurion and those with him, who were guarding Jesus, saw the earthquake and the things that had happened, they feared greatly, saying, “Truly this was the Son of God!”
NKJV.55: And many women who followed Jesus from Galilee, ministering to Him, were there looking on from afar,
NKJV.56: among whom were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joses, and the mother of Zebedee’s sons.
NKJV.57: Now when evening had come, there came a rich man from Arimathea, named Joseph, who himself had also become a disciple of Jesus.
NKJV.58: This man went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus. Then Pilate commanded the body to be given to him.
NKJV.59: When Joseph had taken the body, he wrapped it in a clean linen cloth,
NKJV.60: and laid it in his new tomb which he had hewn out of the rock; and he rolled a large stone against the door of the tomb, and departed.
NKJV.61: And Mary Magdalene was there, and the other Mary, sitting opposite the tomb.
NKJV.62: On the next day, which followed the Day of Preparation, the chief priests and Pharisees gathered together to Pilate,
NKJV.63: saying, “Sir, we remember, while He was still alive, how that deceiver said, ‘After three days I will rise.’
NKJV.64: Therefore command that the tomb be made secure until the third day, lest His disciples come by night and steal Him away, and say to the people, ‘He has risen from the dead.’ So the last deception will be worse than the first.”
NKJV.65: Pilate said to them, “You have a guard; go your way, make it as secure as you know how.”
NKJV.66: So they went and made the tomb secure, sealing the stone and setting the guard.

ESV.1: When morning came, all the chief priests and the elders of the people took counsel against Jesus to put him to death.
ESV.2: And they bound him and led him away and delivered him over to Pilate the governor.
ESV.3: Then when Judas, his betrayer, saw that Jesus  was condemned, he changed his mind and brought back the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and the elders,
ESV.4: saying, “I have sinned by betraying innocent blood.” They said, “What is that to us? See to it yourself.”
ESV.5: And throwing down the pieces of silver into the temple, he departed, and he went and hanged himself.
ESV.6: But the chief priests, taking the pieces of silver, said, “It is not lawful to put them into the treasury, since it is blood money.”
ESV.7: So they took counsel and bought with them the potter's field as a burial place for strangers.
ESV.8: Therefore that field has been called the Field of Blood to this day.
ESV.9: Then was fulfilled what had been spoken by the prophet Jeremiah, saying, “And they took the thirty pieces of silver, the price of him on whom a price had been set by some of the sons of Israel,
ESV.10: and they gave them for the potter's field, as the Lord directed me.”
ESV.11: Now Jesus stood before the governor, and the governor asked him, “Are you the King of the Jews?” Jesus said,  “You have said so.”
ESV.12: But when he was accused by the chief priests and elders, he gave no answer.
ESV.13: Then Pilate said to him, “Do you not hear how many things they testify against you?”
ESV.14: But he gave him no answer, not even to a single charge, so that the governor was greatly amazed.
ESV.15: Now at the feast the governor was accustomed to release for the crowd any one prisoner whom they wanted.
ESV.16: And they had then a notorious prisoner called Barabbas.
ESV.17: So when they had gathered, Pilate said to them, “Whom do you want me to release for you: Barabbas, or Jesus who is called Christ?”
ESV.18: For he knew that it was out of envy that they had delivered him up.
ESV.19: Besides, while he was sitting on the judgment seat, his wife sent word to him, “Have nothing to do with that righteous man, for I have suffered much because of him today in a dream.”
ESV.20: Now the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowd to ask for Barabbas and destroy Jesus.
ESV.21: The governor again said to them, “Which of the two do you want me to release for you?” And they said, “Barabbas.”
ESV.22: Pilate said to them, “Then what shall I do with Jesus who is called Christ?” They all said, “Let him be crucified!”
ESV.23: And he said, “Why? What evil has he done?” But they shouted all the more, “Let him be crucified!”
ESV.24: So when Pilate saw that he was gaining nothing, but rather that a riot was beginning, he took water and washed his hands before the crowd, saying, “I am innocent of this man's blood;  see to it yourselves.”
ESV.25: And all the people answered, “His blood be on us and on our children!”
ESV.26: Then he released for them Barabbas, and having scourged  Jesus, delivered him to be crucified.
ESV.27: Then the soldiers of the governor took Jesus into the governor's headquarters,  and they gathered the whole battalion  before him.
ESV.28: And they stripped him and put a scarlet robe on him,
ESV.29: and twisting together a crown of thorns, they put it on his head and put a reed in his right hand. And kneeling before him, they mocked him, saying, “Hail, King of the Jews!”
ESV.30: And they spit on him and took the reed and struck him on the head.
ESV.31: And when they had mocked him, they stripped him of the robe and put his own clothes on him and led him away to crucify him.
ESV.32: As they went out, they found a man of Cyrene, Simon by name. They compelled this man to carry his cross.
ESV.33: And when they came to a place called Golgotha (which means Place of a Skull),
ESV.34: they offered him wine to drink, mixed with gall, but when he tasted it, he would not drink it.
ESV.35: And when they had crucified him, they divided his garments among them by casting lots.
ESV.36: Then they sat down and kept watch over him there.
ESV.37: And over his head they put the charge against him, which read, “This is Jesus, the King of the Jews.”
ESV.38: Then two robbers were crucified with him, one on the right and one on the left.
ESV.39: And those who passed by derided him, wagging their heads
ESV.40: and saying, “You who would destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, save yourself! If you are the Son of God, come down from the cross.”
ESV.41: So also the chief priests, with the scribes and elders, mocked him, saying,
ESV.42: “He saved others; he cannot save himself. He is the King of Israel; let him come down now from the cross, and we will believe in him.
ESV.43: He trusts in God; let God deliver him now, if he desires him. For he said, ‘I am the Son of God.’”
ESV.44: And the robbers who were crucified with him also reviled him in the same way.
ESV.45: Now from the sixth hour  there was darkness over all the land  until the ninth hour.
ESV.46: And about the ninth hour Jesus cried out with a loud voice, saying,  “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” that is, “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?”
ESV.47: And some of the bystanders, hearing it, said, “This man is calling Elijah.”
ESV.48: And one of them at once ran and took a sponge, filled it with sour wine, and put it on a reed and gave it to him to drink.
ESV.49: But the others said, “Wait, let us see whether Elijah will come to save him.”
ESV.50: And Jesus cried out again with a loud voice and yielded up his spirit.
ESV.51: And behold, the curtain of the temple was torn in two, from top to bottom. And the earth shook, and the rocks were split.
ESV.52: The tombs also were opened. And many bodies of the saints who had fallen asleep were raised,
ESV.53: and coming out of the tombs after his resurrection they went into the holy city and appeared to many.
ESV.54: When the centurion and those who were with him, keeping watch over Jesus, saw the earthquake and what took place, they were filled with awe and said, “Truly this was the Son  of God!”
ESV.55: There were also many women there, looking on from a distance, who had followed Jesus from Galilee, ministering to him,
ESV.56: among whom were Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James and Joseph and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.
ESV.57: When it was evening, there came a rich man from Arimathea, named Joseph, who also was a disciple of Jesus.
ESV.58: He went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus. Then Pilate ordered it to be given to him.
ESV.59: And Joseph took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen shroud
ESV.60: and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had cut in the rock. And he rolled a great stone to the entrance of the tomb and went away.
ESV.61: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were there, sitting opposite the tomb.
ESV.62: The next day, that is, after the day of Preparation, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered before Pilate
ESV.63: and said, “Sir, we remember how that impostor said, while he was still alive, ‘After three days I will rise.’
ESV.64: Therefore order the tomb to be made secure until the third day, lest his disciples go and steal him away and tell the people, ‘He has risen from the dead,’ and the last fraud will be worse than the first.”
ESV.65: Pilate said to them, “You have a guard  of soldiers. Go, make it as secure as you can.”
ESV.66: So they went and made the tomb secure by sealing the stone and setting a guard.

NASB.1: Now when morning came, all the chief priests and the elders of the people conferred together against Jesus to put Him to death;
NASB.2: and they bound Him, and led Him away and delivered Him to Pilate the governor.
NASB.3: Then when Judas, who had betrayed Him, saw that He had been condemned, he felt remorse and returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders,
NASB.4: saying, 'I have sinned by betraying innocent blood.' But they said, 'What is that to us? See [to that] yourself!'
NASB.5: And he threw the pieces of silver into the temple sanctuary and departed; and he went away and hanged himself.
NASB.6: The chief priests took the pieces of silver and said, 'It is not lawful to put them into the temple treasury, since it is the price of blood.'
NASB.7: And they conferred together and with the money bought the Potter's Field as a burial place for strangers.
NASB.8: For this reason that field has been called the Field of Blood to this day.
NASB.9: Then that which was spoken through Jeremiah the prophet was fulfilled: 'AND THEY TOOK THE THIRTY PIECES OF SILVER, THE PRICE OF THE ONE WHOSE PRICE HAD BEEN SET by the sons of Israel;
NASB.10: AND THEY GAVE THEM FOR THE POTTER'S FIELD, AS THE LORD DIRECTED ME.'
NASB.11: Now Jesus stood before the governor, and the governor questioned Him, saying, 'Are You the King of the Jews?' And Jesus said to him, '[It is as] you say.'
NASB.12: And while He was being accused by the chief priests and elders, He did not answer.
NASB.13: Then Pilate said to Him, 'Do You not hear how many things they testify against You?'
NASB.14: And He did not answer him with regard to even a [single] charge, so the governor was quite amazed.
NASB.15: Now at [the] feast the governor was accustomed to release for the people [any] one prisoner whom they wanted.
NASB.16: At that time they were holding a notorious prisoner, called Barabbas.
NASB.17: So when the people gathered together, Pilate said to them, 'Whom do you want me to release for you? Barabbas, or Jesus who is called Christ?'
NASB.18: For he knew that because of envy they had handed Him over.
NASB.19: While he was sitting on the judgment seat, his wife sent him [a message], saying, 'Have nothing to do with that righteous Man; for last night I suffered greatly in a dream because of Him.'
NASB.20: But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowds to ask for Barabbas and to put Jesus to death.
NASB.21: But the governor said to them, 'Which of the two do you want me to release for you?' And they said, 'Barabbas.'
NASB.22: Pilate said to them, 'Then what shall I do with Jesus who is called Christ?' They all said, 'Crucify Him!'
NASB.23: And he said, 'Why, what evil has He done?' But they kept shouting all the more, saying, 'Crucify Him!'
NASB.24: When Pilate saw that he was accomplishing nothing, but rather that a riot was starting, he took water and washed his hands in front of the crowd, saying, 'I am innocent of this Man's blood; see [to that] yourselves.'
NASB.25: And all the people said, 'His blood shall be on us and on our children!'
NASB.26: Then he released Barabbas for them; but after having Jesus scourged, he handed Him over to be crucified.
NASB.27: Then the soldiers of the governor took Jesus into the Praetorium and gathered the whole [Roman] cohort around Him.
NASB.28: They stripped Him and put a scarlet robe on Him.
NASB.29: And after twisting together a crown of thorns, they put it on His head, and a reed in His right hand; and they knelt down before Him and mocked Him, saying, 'Hail, King of the Jews!'
NASB.30: They spat on Him, and took the reed and [began] to beat Him on the head.
NASB.31: After they had mocked Him, they took the [scarlet] robe off Him and put His [own] garments back on Him, and led Him away to crucify Him.
NASB.32: As they were coming out, they found a man of Cyrene named Simon, whom they pressed into service to bear His cross.
NASB.33: And when they came to a place called Golgotha, which means Place of a Skull,
NASB.34: they gave Him wine to drink mixed with gall; and after tasting [it], He was unwilling to drink.
NASB.35: And when they had crucified Him, they divided up His garments among themselves by casting lots.
NASB.36: And sitting down, they [began] to keep watch over Him there.
NASB.37: And above His head they put up the charge against Him which read, 'THIS IS JESUS THE KING OF THE JEWS.'
NASB.38: At that time two robbers were crucified with Him, one on the right and one on the left.
NASB.39: And those passing by were hurling abuse at Him, wagging their heads
NASB.40: and saying, 'You who [are going to] destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, save Yourself! If You are the Son of God, come down from the cross.'
NASB.41: In the same way the chief priests also, along with the scribes and elders, were mocking [Him] and saying,
NASB.42: 'He saved others; He cannot save Himself. He is the King of Israel; let Him now come down from the cross, and we will believe in Him.
NASB.43: 'HE TRUSTS IN GOD; LET GOD RESCUE [Him] now, IF HE DELIGHTS IN HIM; for He said, 'I am the Son of God.''
NASB.44: The robbers who had been crucified with Him were also insulting Him with the same words.
NASB.45: Now from the sixth hour darkness fell upon all the land until the ninth hour.
NASB.46: About the ninth hour Jesus cried out with a loud voice, saying, 'ELI, ELI, LAMA SABACHTHANI?' that is, 'MY GOD, MY GOD, WHY HAVE YOU FORSAKEN ME?'
NASB.47: And some of those who were standing there, when they heard it, [began] saying, 'This man is calling for Elijah.'
NASB.48: Immediately one of them ran, and taking a sponge, he filled it with sour wine and put it on a reed, and gave Him a drink.
NASB.49: But the rest [of them] said, 'Let us see whether Elijah will come to save Him.'
NASB.50: And Jesus cried out again with a loud voice, and yielded up His spirit.
NASB.51: And behold, the veil of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom; and the earth shook and the rocks were split.
NASB.52: The tombs were opened, and many bodies of the saints who had fallen asleep were raised;
NASB.53: and coming out of the tombs after His resurrection they entered the holy city and appeared to many.
NASB.54: Now the centurion, and those who were with him keeping guard over Jesus, when they saw the earthquake and the things that were happening, became very frightened and said, 'Truly this was the Son of God!'
NASB.55: Many women were there looking on from a distance, who had followed Jesus from Galilee while ministering to Him.
NASB.56: Among them was Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.
NASB.57: When it was evening, there came a rich man from Arimathea, named Joseph, who himself had also become a disciple of Jesus.
NASB.58: This man went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus. Then Pilate ordered it to be given [to him].
NASB.59: And Joseph took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth,
NASB.60: and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had hewn out in the rock; and he rolled a large stone against the entrance of the tomb and went away.
NASB.61: And Mary Magdalene was there, and the other Mary, sitting opposite the grave.
NASB.62: Now on the next day, the day after the preparation, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered together with Pilate,
NASB.63: and said, 'Sir, we remember that when He was still alive that deceiver said, 'After three days I [am to] rise again.'
NASB.64: 'Therefore, give orders for the grave to be made secure until the third day, otherwise His disciples may come and steal Him away and say to the people, 'He has risen from the dead,' and the last deception will be worse than the first.'
NASB.65: Pilate said to them, 'You have a guard; go, make it [as] secure as you know how.'
NASB.66: And they went and made the grave secure, and along with the guard they set a seal on the stone.

NLT.1: Very early in the morning the leading priests and the elders of the people met again to lay plans for putting Jesus to death.
NLT.2: Then they bound him, led him away, and took him to Pilate, the Roman governor.
NLT.3: When Judas, who had betrayed him, realized that Jesus had been condemned to die, he was filled with remorse. So he took the thirty pieces of silver back to the leading priests and the elders.
NLT.4: “I have sinned,” he declared, “for I have betrayed an innocent man.” “What do we care?” they retorted. “That’s your problem.”
NLT.5: Then Judas threw the silver coins down in the Temple and went out and hanged himself.
NLT.6: The leading priests picked up the coins. “It wouldn’t be right to put this money in the Temple treasury,” they said, “since it was payment for murder.”
NLT.7: After some discussion they finally decided to buy the potter’s field, and they made it into a cemetery for foreigners.
NLT.8: That is why the field is still called the Field of Blood.
NLT.9:
This fulfilled the prophecy of Jeremiah that says, “They took the thirty pieces of silver —
the price at which he was valued by the people of Israel,
NLT.10:
and purchased the potter’s field,
as the LORD directed. ”
NLT.11: Now Jesus was standing before Pilate, the Roman governor. “Are you the king of the Jews?” the governor asked him. Jesus replied, “You have said it.”
NLT.12: But when the leading priests and the elders made their accusations against him, Jesus remained silent.
NLT.13: “Don’t you hear all these charges they are bringing against you?” Pilate demanded.
NLT.14: But Jesus made no response to any of the charges, much to the governor’s surprise.
NLT.15: Now it was the governor’s custom each year during the Passover celebration to release one prisoner to the crowd — anyone they wanted.
NLT.16: This year there was a notorious prisoner, a man named Barabbas.
NLT.17: As the crowds gathered before Pilate’s house that morning, he asked them, “Which one do you want me to release to you — Barabbas, or Jesus who is called the Messiah?”
NLT.18: (He knew very well that the religious leaders had arrested Jesus out of envy.)
NLT.19: Just then, as Pilate was sitting on the judgment seat, his wife sent him this message: “Leave that innocent man alone. I suffered through a terrible nightmare about him last night.”
NLT.20: Meanwhile, the leading priests and the elders persuaded the crowd to ask for Barabbas to be released and for Jesus to be put to death.
NLT.21: So the governor asked again, “Which of these two do you want me to release to you?” The crowd shouted back, “Barabbas!”
NLT.22: Pilate responded, “Then what should I do with Jesus who is called the Messiah?” They shouted back, “Crucify him!”
NLT.23: “Why?” Pilate demanded. “What crime has he committed?” But the mob roared even louder, “Crucify him!”
NLT.24: Pilate saw that he wasn’t getting anywhere and that a riot was developing. So he sent for a bowl of water and washed his hands before the crowd, saying, “I am innocent of this man’s blood. The responsibility is yours!”
NLT.25: And all the people yelled back, “We will take responsibility for his death — we and our children!”
NLT.26: So Pilate released Barabbas to them. He ordered Jesus flogged with a lead-tipped whip, then turned him over to the Roman soldiers to be crucified.
NLT.27: Some of the governor’s soldiers took Jesus into their headquarters and called out the entire regiment.
NLT.28: They stripped him and put a scarlet robe on him.
NLT.29: They wove thorn branches into a crown and put it on his head, and they placed a reed stick in his right hand as a scepter. Then they knelt before him in mockery and taunted, “Hail! King of the Jews!”
NLT.30: And they spit on him and grabbed the stick and struck him on the head with it.
NLT.31: When they were finally tired of mocking him, they took off the robe and put his own clothes on him again. Then they led him away to be crucified.
NLT.32: Along the way, they came across a man named Simon, who was from Cyrene, and the soldiers forced him to carry Jesus’ cross.
NLT.33: And they went out to a place called Golgotha (which means “Place of the Skull”).
NLT.34: The soldiers gave Jesus wine mixed with bitter gall, but when he had tasted it, he refused to drink it.
NLT.35: After they had nailed him to the cross, the soldiers gambled for his clothes by throwing dice.
NLT.36: Then they sat around and kept guard as he hung there.
NLT.37: A sign was fastened above Jesus’ head, announcing the charge against him. It read: “This is Jesus, the King of the Jews.”
NLT.38: Two revolutionaries were crucified with him, one on his right and one on his left.
NLT.39: The people passing by shouted abuse, shaking their heads in mockery.
NLT.40: “Look at you now!” they yelled at him. “You said you were going to destroy the Temple and rebuild it in three days. Well then, if you are the Son of God, save yourself and come down from the cross!”
NLT.41: The leading priests, the teachers of religious law, and the elders also mocked Jesus.
NLT.42: “He saved others,” they scoffed, “but he can’t save himself! So he is the King of Israel, is he? Let him come down from the cross right now, and we will believe in him!
NLT.43: He trusted God, so let God rescue him now if he wants him! For he said, ‘I am the Son of God.’”
NLT.44: Even the revolutionaries who were crucified with him ridiculed him in the same way.
NLT.45: At noon, darkness fell across the whole land until three o’clock.
NLT.46: At about three o’clock, Jesus called out with a loud voice, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” which means “My God, my God, why have you abandoned me?”
NLT.47: Some of the bystanders misunderstood and thought he was calling for the prophet Elijah.
NLT.48: One of them ran and filled a sponge with sour wine, holding it up to him on a reed stick so he could drink.
NLT.49: But the rest said, “Wait! Let’s see whether Elijah comes to save him.”
NLT.50: Then Jesus shouted out again, and he released his spirit.
NLT.51: At that moment the curtain in the sanctuary of the Temple was torn in two, from top to bottom. The earth shook, rocks split apart,
NLT.52: and tombs opened. The bodies of many godly men and women who had died were raised from the dead.
NLT.53: They left the cemetery after Jesus’ resurrection, went into the holy city of Jerusalem, and appeared to many people.
NLT.54: The Roman officer and the other soldiers at the crucifixion were terrified by the earthquake and all that had happened. They said, “This man truly was the Son of God!”
NLT.55: And many women who had come from Galilee with Jesus to care for him were watching from a distance.
NLT.56: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary (the mother of James and Joseph), and the mother of James and John, the sons of Zebedee.
NLT.57: As evening approached, Joseph, a rich man from Arimathea who had become a follower of Jesus,
NLT.58: went to Pilate and asked for Jesus’ body. And Pilate issued an order to release it to him.
NLT.59: Joseph took the body and wrapped it in a long sheet of clean linen cloth.
NLT.60: He placed it in his own new tomb, which had been carved out of the rock. Then he rolled a great stone across the entrance and left.
NLT.61: Both Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting across from the tomb and watching.
NLT.62: The next day, on the Sabbath, the leading priests and Pharisees went to see Pilate.
NLT.63: They told him, “Sir, we remember what that deceiver once said while he was still alive: ‘After three days I will rise from the dead.’
NLT.64: So we request that you seal the tomb until the third day. This will prevent his disciples from coming and stealing his body and then telling everyone he was raised from the dead! If that happens, we’ll be worse off than we were at first.”
NLT.65: Pilate replied, “Take guards and secure it the best you can.”
NLT.66: So they sealed the tomb and posted guards to protect it.

WEB.1: Now when morning had come, all the chief priests and the elders of the people took counsel against Jesus to put him to death:
WEB.2: and they bound him, and led him away, and delivered him up to Pontius Pilate, the governor.
WEB.3: Then Judas, who betrayed him, when he saw that Jesus was condemned, felt remorse, and brought back the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders,
WEB.4: saying, “I have sinned in that I betrayed innocent blood.” But they said, “What is that to us? You see to it.”
WEB.5: He threw down the pieces of silver in the sanctuary, and departed. He went away and hanged himself.
WEB.6: The chief priests took the pieces of silver, and said, “It’s not lawful to put them into the treasury, since it is the price of blood.”
WEB.7: They took counsel, and bought the potter’s field with them, to bury strangers in.
WEB.8: Therefore that field was called “The Field of Blood” to this day.
WEB.9: Then that which was spoken through Jeremiah  the prophet was fulfilled, saying, “They took the thirty pieces of silver, the price of him upon whom a price had been set, whom some of the children of Israel priced,
WEB.10: and they gave them for the potter’s field, as the Lord commanded me.”
WEB.11: Now Jesus stood before the governor: and the governor asked him, saying, “Are you the King of the Jews?” Jesus said to him, “So you say.”
WEB.12: When he was accused by the chief priests and elders, he answered nothing.
WEB.13: Then Pilate said to him, “Don’t you hear how many things they testify against you?”
WEB.14: He gave him no answer, not even one word, so that the governor marveled greatly.
WEB.15: Now at the feast the governor was accustomed to release to the multitude one prisoner, whom they desired.
WEB.16: They had then a notable prisoner, called Barabbas.
WEB.17: When therefore they were gathered together, Pilate said to them, “Whom do you want me to release to you? Barabbas, or Jesus, who is called Christ?”
WEB.18: For he knew that because of envy they had delivered him up.
WEB.19: While he was sitting on the judgment seat, his wife sent to him, saying, “Have nothing to do with that righteous man, for I have suffered many things today in a dream because of him.”
WEB.20: Now the chief priests and the elders persuaded the multitudes to ask for Barabbas, and destroy Jesus.
WEB.21: But the governor answered them, “Which of the two do you want me to release to you?” They said, “Barabbas!”
WEB.22: Pilate said to them, “What then shall I do to Jesus, who is called Christ?” They all said to him, “Let him be crucified!”
WEB.23: But the governor said, “Why? What evil has he done?” But they cried out exceedingly, saying, “Let him be crucified!”
WEB.24: So when Pilate saw that nothing was being gained, but rather that a disturbance was starting, he took water, and washed his hands before the multitude, saying, “I am innocent of the blood of this righteous person. You see to it.”
WEB.25: All the people answered, “May his blood be on us, and on our children!”
WEB.26: Then he released to them Barabbas, but Jesus he flogged and delivered to be crucified.
WEB.27: Then the governor’s soldiers took Jesus into the Praetorium, and gathered the whole garrison together against him.
WEB.28: They stripped him, and put a scarlet robe on him.
WEB.29: They braided a crown of thorns and put it on his head, and a reed in his right hand; and they kneeled down before him, and mocked him, saying, “Hail, King of the Jews!”
WEB.30: They spat on him, and took the reed and struck him on the head.
WEB.31: When they had mocked him, they took the robe off of him, and put his clothes on him, and led him away to crucify him.
WEB.32: As they came out, they found a man of Cyrene, Simon by name, and they compelled him to go with them, that he might carry his cross.
WEB.33: When they came to a place called “Golgotha”, that is to say, “The place of a skull,”
WEB.34: they gave him sour wine  to drink mixed with gall. When he had tasted it, he would not drink.
WEB.35: When they had crucified him, they divided his clothing among them, casting lots,
WEB.36: and they sat and watched him there.
WEB.37: They set up over his head the accusation against him written, “THIS IS JESUS, THE KING OF THE JEWS.”
WEB.38: Then there were two robbers crucified with him, one on his right hand and one on the left.
WEB.39: Those who passed by blasphemed him, wagging their heads,
WEB.40: and saying, “You who destroy the temple, and build it in three days, save yourself! If you are the Son of God, come down from the cross!”
WEB.41: Likewise the chief priests also mocking, with the scribes, the Pharisees,  and the elders, said,
WEB.42: “He saved others, but he can’t save himself. If he is the King of Israel, let him come down from the cross now, and we will believe in him.
WEB.43: He trusts in God. Let God deliver him now, if he wants him; for he said, ‘I am the Son of God.’”
WEB.44: The robbers also who were crucified with him cast on him the same reproach.
WEB.45: Now from the sixth hour  there was darkness over all the land until the ninth hour.
WEB.46: About the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, “Eli, Eli, lima  sabachthani?”  That is, “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?”
WEB.47: Some of them who stood there, when they heard it, said, “This man is calling Elijah.”
WEB.48: Immediately one of them ran, and took a sponge, and filled it with vinegar, and put it on a reed, and gave him a drink.
WEB.49: The rest said, “Let him be. Let’s see whether Elijah comes to save him.”
WEB.50: Jesus cried again with a loud voice, and yielded up his spirit.
WEB.51: Behold, the veil of the temple was torn in two from the top to the bottom. The earth quaked and the rocks were split.
WEB.52: The tombs were opened, and many bodies of the saints who had fallen asleep were raised;
WEB.53: and coming out of the tombs after his resurrection, they entered into the holy city and appeared to many.
WEB.54: Now the centurion, and those who were with him watching Jesus, when they saw the earthquake, and the things that were done, feared exceedingly, saying, “Truly this was the Son of God.”
WEB.55: Many women were there watching from afar, who had followed Jesus from Galilee, serving him.
WEB.56: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joses, and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.
WEB.57: When evening had come, a rich man from Arimathaea, named Joseph, who himself was also Jesus’ disciple came.
WEB.58: This man went to Pilate, and asked for Jesus’ body. Then Pilate commanded the body to be given up.
WEB.59: Joseph took the body, and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth,
WEB.60: and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had cut out in the rock, and he rolled a great stone to the door of the tomb, and departed.
WEB.61: Mary Magdalene was there, and the other Mary, sitting opposite the tomb.
WEB.62: Now on the next day, which was the day after the Preparation Day, the chief priests and the Pharisees were gathered together to Pilate,
WEB.63: saying, “Sir, we remember what that deceiver said while he was still alive: ‘After three days I will rise again.’
WEB.64: Command therefore that the tomb be made secure until the third day, lest perhaps his disciples come at night and steal him away, and tell the people, ‘He is risen from the dead;’ and the last deception will be worse than the first.”
WEB.65: Pilate said to them, “You have a guard. Go, make it as secure as you can.”
WEB.66: So they went with the guard and made the tomb secure, sealing the stone.

AMP.1: When it was morning, all the chief priests and the elders of the people (Sanhedrin, Jewish High Court) conferred together against Jesus, [plotting how] to put Him to death [since under Roman rule they had no power to execute anyone];
AMP.2: so they bound Him, and led Him away and handed Him over to Pilate the governor [of Judea, who had the authority to condemn prisoners to death].
AMP.3: When Judas, His betrayer, saw that Jesus was condemned, he was gripped with remorse and returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and the elders,
AMP.4: saying, "I have sinned by betraying innocent blood." They replied, "What is that to us? See to that yourself!"
AMP.5: And throwing the pieces of silver into the temple sanctuary, he left; and went away and hanged himself.
AMP.6: The chief priests, picking up the pieces of silver, said, "It is not lawful to put these in the treasury [of the temple], because it is the price of blood."
AMP.7: So after consultation they used the money to buy the Potter's Field as a burial place for strangers.
AMP.8: Therefore that piece of ground has been called the Field of Blood to this day.
AMP.9: Then the words spoken by Jeremiah the prophet were fulfilled: "<e>And they took the thirty pieces of silver, the price of Him on whom a price had been set</e> by the sons of Israel;
AMP.10: <e>and they gave them for the Potter's Field, as the Lord directed me</e>."
AMP.11: Now Jesus stood before [Pilate] the governor, and the governor asked Him, "Are you the King of the Jews?" [In affirmation] Jesus said to him, "It is as you say."
AMP.12: But when the charges were brought against Him by the chief priests and elders, He did not answer.
AMP.13: Then Pilate said to Him, "Do You not hear how many things they are testifying against You?"
AMP.14: But Jesus did not reply to him, not even to a single accusation, so that the governor was greatly astonished.
AMP.15: Now at the feast [of the Passover] the governor was in the habit of setting free any one prisoner whom the people chose.
AMP.16: And at that time they were holding a notorious prisoner [guilty of insurrection and murder], called Barabbas.
AMP.17: So when they had assembled [for this purpose], Pilate said to them, "Whom do you want me to set free for you? Barabbas, or Jesus who is called Christ?"
AMP.18: For Pilate knew that it was because of jealousy that the chief priests and elders had handed Jesus over to him.
AMP.19: While he was seated on the judgment seat, his wife sent him a message, saying, "Have nothing to do with that righteous and innocent Man; for last night I suffered greatly in a dream because of Him."
AMP.20: But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowds to ask for Barabbas and to put Jesus to death.
AMP.21: The governor said to them, "Which of the two do you wish me to set free for you?" And they said, "Barabbas."
AMP.22: Pilate said to them, "Then what shall I do with Jesus who is called Christ?" They all replied, "Let Him be crucified!"
AMP.23: And he said, "Why, what has He done that is evil?" But they continued shouting all the louder, "Let Him be crucified!"
AMP.24: So when Pilate saw that he was getting nowhere, but rather that a riot was breaking out, he took water and washed his hands [to ceremonially cleanse himself of guilt] in the presence of the crowd, saying, "I am innocent of this [righteous] Man's blood; see to that yourselves."
AMP.25: And all the people answered, "Let [the responsibility for] His blood be on us and on our children!"
AMP.26: So he set Barabbas free for them; but after having Jesus severely whipped (scourged), he handed Him over to be crucified.
AMP.27: Then the governor's soldiers took Jesus into the Praetorium, and they gathered the whole Roman cohort around Him.
AMP.28: They stripped him and put a scarlet robe on Him [as a king's robe].
AMP.29: And after twisting together a crown of thorns, they put it on His head, and put a reed in His right hand [as a scepter]. Kneeling before Him, they ridiculed Him, saying, "Hail (rejoice), King of the Jews!"
AMP.30: They spat on Him, and took the reed and struck Him repeatedly on the head.
AMP.31: After they finished ridiculing Him, they stripped Him of the scarlet robe and put His own clothes on Him, and led Him away to crucify Him.
AMP.32: Now as they were coming out, they found a man of Cyrene named Simon, whom they forced into service to carry the cross of Jesus.
AMP.33: And when they came to a place called Golgotha, which means Place of a Skull,
AMP.34: they offered Him wine mixed with gall (myrrh, a bitter-tasting narcotic) to drink; but after tasting it, He refused to drink it.
AMP.35: And when they had crucified Him, they divided His clothes among them by casting lots.
AMP.36: Then sitting down there, they began to keep watch over Him [to guard against any rescue attempt].
AMP.37: And above His head they put the accusation against Him which read, " THIS IS JESUS THE KING OF THE JEWS."
AMP.38: At the same time two robbers were crucified with Jesus, one on the right and one on the left.
AMP.39: Those who passed by were hurling abuse at Him and jeering at Him, wagging their heads [in scorn and ridicule],
AMP.40: and they said [tauntingly], "You who would destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, save Yourself [from death]! If You are the Son of God, come down from the cross."
AMP.41: In the same way the chief priests also, along with the scribes and elders, mocked Him, saying,

NET.17: So after they had assembled, Pilate said to them, “Whom do you want me to release for you, Jesus Barabbas or Jesus who is called the Christ?”
NET.18: (For he knew that they had handed him over because of envy.)
NET.19: As he was sitting on the judgment seat, his wife sent a message to him: “Have nothing to do with that innocent man; I have suffered greatly as a result of a dream about him today.”
NET.20: But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowds to ask for Barabbas and to have Jesus killed.
NET.21: The governor asked them, “Which of the two do you want me to release for you?” And they said, “Barabbas!”
NET.22: Pilate said to them, “Then what should I do with Jesus who is called the Christ?” They all said, “Crucify him!”
NET.23: He asked, “Why? What wrong has he done?” But they shouted more insistently, “Crucify him!”
NET.24: When Pilate saw that he could do nothing, but that instead a riot was starting, he took some water, washed his hands before the crowd and said, “I am innocent of this man’s blood. You take care of it yourselves!”
NET.25: In reply all the people said, “Let his blood be on us and on our children!”
NET.26: Then he released Barabbas for them. But after he had Jesus flogged, he handed him over to be crucified.
NET.27: Then the governor’s soldiers took Jesus into the governor’s residence and gathered the whole cohort around him.
NET.28: They stripped him and put a scarlet robe around him,
NET.29: and after braiding a crown of thorns, they put it on his head. They put a staff in his right hand, and kneeling down before him, they mocked him: “Hail, king of the Jews!”
NET.30: They spat on him and took the staff and struck him repeatedly on the head.
NET.31: When they had mocked him, they stripped him of the robe and put his own clothes back on him. Then they led him away to crucify him.
NET.32: As they were going out, they found a man from Cyrene named Simon, whom they forced to carry his cross.
NET.33: They came to a place called Golgotha (which means “Place of the Skull”)
NET.34: and offered Jesus wine mixed with gall to drink. But after tasting it, he would not drink it.
NET.35: When they had crucified him, they divided his clothes by throwing dice.
NET.36: Then they sat down and kept guard over him there.
NET.37: Above his head they put the charge against him, which read: “This is Jesus, the king of the Jews.”
NET.38: Then two outlaws were crucified with him, one on his right and one on his left.
NET.39: Those who passed by defamed him, shaking their heads
NET.40: and saying, “You who can destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, save yourself! If you are God’s Son, come down from the cross!”
NET.41: In the same way even the chief priests — together with the experts in the law and elders — were mocking him:
NET.42: “He saved others, but he cannot save himself! He is the king of Israel! If he comes down now from the cross, we will believe in him!
NET.43: He trusts in God — let God, if he wants to, deliver him now because he said, ‘I am God’s Son’!”
NET.44: The robbers who were crucified with him also spoke abusively to him.
NET.45: Now from noon until three, darkness came over all the land.
NET.46: At about three o’clock Jesus shouted with a loud voice, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” that is, “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?”
NET.47: When some of the bystanders heard it, they said, “This man is calling for Elijah.”
NET.48: Immediately one of them ran and got a sponge, filled it with sour wine, put it on a stick, and gave it to him to drink.
NET.49: But the rest said, “Leave him alone! Let’s see if Elijah will come to save him.”
NET.50: Then Jesus cried out again with a loud voice and gave up his spirit.
NET.51: Just then the temple curtain was torn in two, from top to bottom. The earth shook and the rocks were split apart.
NET.52: And tombs were opened, and the bodies of many saints who had died were raised.
NET.53: (They came out of the tombs after his resurrection and went into the holy city and appeared to many people.)
NET.54: Now when the centurion and those with him who were guarding Jesus saw the earthquake and what took place, they were extremely terrified and said, “Truly this one was God’s Son!”
NET.55: Many women who had followed Jesus from Galilee and given him support were also there, watching from a distance.
NET.56: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.
NET.57: Now when it was evening, there came a rich man from Arimathea, named Joseph, who was also a disciple of Jesus.
NET.58: He went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus. Then Pilate ordered that it be given to him.
NET.59: Joseph took the body, wrapped it in a clean linen cloth,
NET.60: and placed it in his own new tomb that he had cut in the rock. Then he rolled a great stone across the entrance of the tomb and went away.
NET.61: (Now Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting there, opposite the tomb.)
NET.62: The next day (which is after the day of preparation) the chief priests and the Pharisees assembled before Pilate
NET.63: and said, “Sir, we remember that while that deceiver was still alive he said, ‘After three days I will rise again.’
NET.64: So give orders to secure the tomb until the third day. Otherwise his disciples may come and steal his body and say to the people, ‘He has been raised from the dead,’ and the last deception will be worse than the first.”
NET.65: Pilate said to them, “Take a guard of soldiers. Go and make it as secure as you can.”
NET.66: So they went with the soldiers of the guard and made the tomb secure by sealing the stone.
